title: Default usage of registry.conf and base image name
body:
I may simply need clarification on a configuration item, or this may fall under an enhancement. I would like to set the default tag to something other than "docker.io/library/..."

**Description**

`buildah tag <host>.<domain>:<port>/<folder>/<image>:<tag>`
versus 
`buildah tag <image>:<tag>`

By default the image is tagged as docker.io/library irrespective of the contents of /etc/containers/registries.conf

Ideally there would be a way to tag the images with a default base name that is not docker.io.

comments.body:
Also, I can't seem to find good documentation on the formatting behind /etc/containers/mounts.conf. Can anyone point me at it?

[UPDATE] Was able to infer from https://git.centos.org/commitdiff/rpms!skopeo.git/5849f417904aa2ddc8b422f1935dc4e60347908b#nb7cde9d8a3bd12cb25f86b28323df58fe867293b.

@kbaegis Thanks for the issue report.  I'd thought the /etc/containers/registries.conf would have ruled there if it was present.  Will have to play around with that further.  Glad you found the mount info!
@TomSweeneyRedHat Did you get the same problem?
Haven't tested in the past few days, but have seen this in the past.
Could you check if podman has the same problem or if this is fixed in podman. @baude might have done something around this.
Will do.  I'm pretty sure it's OK in Podman, either @baude or maybe @umohnani8 worked on this.
I am about to open a PR on documenting the mounts.conf file.
We now are creating content as localhost/IMAGE.
